description: User directive (verbatim, this session): 'first we need a script that will use nlp processing to pre-identify candidates needing full agent treatment for validating / de-hedging / correcting, for all substrate items, not just gaps.' Write a read-only script (scripts/, matching this repo's scan-comment-citations.ts precedent) that scans EVERY block kind declared in the active substrate's config (derived from config.block_kinds -- not a hardcoded block list, per this project's derive-from-registry convention) and every free-text prose field of each item (derived from each block's schema -- string/string[] fields such as description, proposed_resolution, acceptance_criteria entries, context, consequences, impact -- not hardcoded per block kind), applying heuristic pattern matching to flag hedge/fork/incompleteness markers in three categories: (1) enumerated-option forks ("or", "and/or", "either...or", lettered/numbered option lists like "(a)...(b)...(c)"); (2) explicit deferral language ("as a decision", "TBD", "to be determined", "not yet decided", "open question", "user's call", "user scope call", "up to the user", "undetermined"); (3) vague/modal hedging ("might", "could", "possibly", "may need to", "later", "eventually"). Output a single machine-readable JSON report (timestamped path) listing, per matched item: block, id, field, matched category, and the matching snippet with surrounding context -- this becomes the candidate list an orchestrator works through with the gap-fork-provenance-audit-brief-template (analysis/2026-07-11-gap-fork-provenance-audit-brief-template.md) or its generalization to other block kinds. The script itself does not correct anything -- pre-identification only.
acceptance_criteria: Script discovers block kinds and their schemas dynamically from the active substrate's config (block_kinds, schema field definitions) -- no hardcoded list of block names or field names anywhere in the script.
Scans every string/string[]-typed prose field across every item in every discovered block.
Flags matches for all three marker categories (enumerated-option forks, explicit-deferral phrases, vague/modal hedging) with the matched snippet plus surrounding sentence context.
Output is a single machine-readable JSON file at a timestamped path; the script makes zero substrate writes (read-only).
Running it against the current substrate's framework-gaps block produces a report that includes FGAP-125's still-unaudited proposed_resolution or-clause as a known true-positive sanity check.
Unit tests cover each heuristic marker category against synthetic true-positive and true-negative fixtures (tsx --test, matching this project's testing convention).
